2603.14409.title: PGcGAN: Pathological Gait-Conditioned GAN for Human Gait Synthesis
2603.14409.paper_summary: Pathological gait analysis is constrained by limited and variable clinical datasets, which restrict the modeling of diverse gait impairments. To address this challenge, we propose a Pathological Gait-conditioned Generative Adversarial Network (PGcGAN) that synthesises pathology-specific gait sequences directly from observed 3D pose keypoint trajectories data. The framework incorporates one-hot encoded pathology labels within both the generator and discriminator, enabling controlled synthesis across six gait categories. The generator adopts a conditional autoencoder architecture trained with adversarial and reconstruction objectives to preserve structural and temporal gait characteristics. Experiments on the Pathological Gait Dataset demonstrate strong alignment between real and synthetic sequences through PCA and t-SNE analyses, visual kinematic inspection, and downstream classification tasks. Augmenting real data with synthetic sequences improved pathological gait recognition across GRU, LSTM, and CNN models, indicating that pathology-conditioned gait synthesis can effectively support data augmentation in pathological gait analysis.
2603.14409.paper_summary_zh: 病理步態分析受到有限且變化多端的臨床數據集的限制，這限制了對多樣步態障礙的建模。為了解決這一挑戰，我們提出了一種病理步態條件生成對抗網絡（PGcGAN），該網絡直接從觀察到的3D姿勢關鍵點軌跡數據合成病理特定的步態序列。該框架在生成器和判別器中都納入了獨熱編碼的病理標籤，使得在六種步態類別之間進行受控合成成為可能。生成器採用條件自編碼器架構，並通過對抗性和重建目標進行訓練，以保留結構和時間步態特徵。在病理步態數據集上的實驗通過PCA和t-SNE分析、視覺運動學檢查以及下游分類任務展示了真實序列和合成序列之間的強對齊。用合成序列增強真實數據改善了GRU、LSTM和CNN模型的病理步態識別，這表明病理條件下的步態合成可以有效支持病理步態分析中的數據增強。
2603.14409.author: Mritula Chandrasekaran et.al.
2603.14409.authors: Mritula Chandrasekaran, Sanket Kachole, Jarek Francik, Dimitrios Makris
